reviews.review_text:
This is one of the best, if not the best beginner friendly fighting game out there:
- The tutorial teaches important concepts and fundamentals beyond just the mechanics
- The character guides explain the flow chart and game plan of the characters
- The training settings offer a vast array of tools, including slowing down the time, so you can troubleshoot the combos and inputs
- A huge player base ensuring you can always find someone of your level to play against

All this make this a great experience for anyone unsure if fighting games are for they or not. Besides the matches are highly enjoyable, with plenty of options and skill expressions.

The graphics is great and vibrant and for the players not inclined to MP, the World Tour mode is easily 50 hours of SP fun, plus very good combo trials, arcade mode, V-Battle and Avatar battles are enough to keep anyone entertained for hundreds of hours.

Honestly one of my favorite fighting games of all time, but i can give this game a good review seeing how the disconnect between the developers and the fans is so big.

The content is coming out 3x slower than in Street Fighter 5, the devs communicate nothing but empty promises, "We hear the concerns about lack of content" and then they proceed to do nothing.

No balance patch, no super street fighter 6, then they proceed to drop 4 newcomers, which is not a problem in itself, but with no new stages and no new system changes.

This is the the most underwhelming season yet which is extremely unfortunate as up until this point I have praised this game for good season pass choices, and Capcom hadn't ♥♥♥♥♥♥ up to this degree on really anything yet. This is a concerning sign that the devs may be turning from making actual good content into cost cutting and asset flipping to milk as much money as possible without giving a ♥♥♥♥ about the playerbase since they are miles ahead of their only competition in Tekken and MK.

My first fighting game! Visually beautiful with fluid animation. The inputs feel clean, smooth, and responsive. The in-game hub is also really nice; I got to meet a lot of cool people there. In The open-world mode, you can mix and match abilities, and there are some fun things you can do there too. Overall, it’s a solid game. My only gripe would be the time between updates and content.

I wanted to return and catch up on all the dlcs. I'm not spending another 50 in my currency to do so. 75 if you include the new year 4 pass.

It's too much. 50 for what is essentially 8 new characters is ridiculous, 75 for 12. 

Is 8 characters worth buying almost the entire base game money wise again? No it's not and I'm tired of "Street Fighter Championship Edition" or whatever being the fix. Just make it not so rediculous please.

I can literally buy a whole new game for these prices, 8 characters is not worth a whole new game. Sort it out.
Great game but the DLC is way too overpriced. It is a 4 years old game, come capcom you can do better.

it is very clear the direction the game is going: the explosive damage, easy abuse of mechanics, heavy guessing and dissapointing year 4 pass indicates they want more of the casual crowd in the game, I love newcomers, SFV did it right with zeku, menat and Abigail (zeku being one of my favs of all time)  but besides Bosch (that had participation in world tour) the rest don't click, year 4 pass has way LESS content for the same price, we don't have any of the OGs returning and Tifa is in probably because the remake will line up with her release, she does not belong in SF and even if she is in the roster at least try to balance the roster to please everyone, disagreements will always exist but this new year pass clearly only chose one side of the community: the one that barely plays the game.

This game is massively overpriced, almost feels like a f2p game pushing microtransactions down your throat, except that you actually have to pay full price to access it in the first place. To access the game and all the characters without any "ultimate" extras it costs about 150 euros. It does not give me 150 euro worth of value.

The fighting is nice and the graphics are great, however the hip-hop style music in the background is cringe and makes me want to mute the whole game.

I can't lab against DLC characters, which I will see frequently in ranked without paying more than the base game is worth? Capcom, you have truly surpassed even the most hardcore paint sniffers with your brain damage.

Please, get bent.

When the game first released, it was such a welcome treat after the disaster that was Street Fighter 5, even after Capcom fixed its issues. The best time to play this game was its initial release window. The game just got updated less and less unless it was something they could monetize. My hope to finally make a return to the game was Season 4. Not a single veteran Street Fighter character returns and they shoved Tifa into this. Guess its time to stop caring and move on. Maybe if they turn things around, I will update my review. As a street fighter player since i was 4-5 years old, this hurts. The only good thing to come out of Season 4, will be the Tifa mods if you're into that sort of thing.

Fun and accessible game with horrible monetisation and a bad live service 

The game costs around $50 depending on what edition you get, microtransactions buy single characters or costumes for roughly $5 each, character colours, profile customisation and stages are bought with either microtransactions or an in game currency that is given out in drips and drabs. There are 2 kinds of season passes, a character pass for around $25 that gives you 4 characters as they are released throughout the game's year (slowly), and an ultimate pass for around $40 that gives you what I just mentioned as well as their costumes, colours and whatever stages Capcom decides to give out (we'll get there.) If you want to get good at the game, you have to train against and learn character movesets (labbing,) in other words if you want to get good at the game you have to buy all of the game's characters. Base game and 4 character passes, that's around $150 if you bought the game on release, in my opinion it's indefensible. 

Also, as of the time I'm writing this, the passes are now giving you less content for the same price since World Tour mode is no longer going to have content past year 3 and there will be no new stage in year 4. 

It's a very fun game that is not being supported enough by Capcom whilst trying to get as much money from you as they can

I held out writing a review until I saw how the game develops, but now I'll list my pros and cons, coming out in the negative in the end:

+ nice graphics
+ easy to learn controlls
+ I love M. Bison

- More and more characters DLC only
- NO possibility to rent a character before purchase without tickets!!
- Please fire the designer who thought of this menu structure with the different layers!
- more and more of a money grab the more the game develops.

Recommend? I guess, but barely. This game isn't groundbreaking. It doesn't look the best. It doesn't fight the best. This avatar mode, which is cool, don't get me wrong, is a clear way for them to try and get you to purchase more garbage you won't likely want, and certainly won't need. Dude it cracks me up they are trying to get you to purchase these pieces of clothing that clip into each other horribly, so not only is it sort of scummy, it's not even good.

This game is expensive for what it is dude. I mean it is ABSOLUTELY not worth full price, at all. It's been out since 2023 and it's roster still don't feel stacked, and to even get what is available will cost you extra. 2026 now and still no definite edition out, so you're going to have to shell out extra if you're say, a Sagat player, which thankfully I am not. And from what I am seeing, NO, you can't buy characters one by one if you're only interested in one. No, you have to buy the whole over priced pack to use them.

This game got a lot going against it, and I adore fighting games, and love street fighter, been playing it since I was a little kid. The only reason this gets a recommendation at all is because I do adore the characters, and the fighting is fun, but this game was as close as it gets to a thumbs down. Get these god awful battle passes out of our fighting games. Worlds got enough garbage thanks.

The game has been boring and awfully unbalanced for like two seasons now, yet crapcom is still ignorant of that fact and as it stands they don't really seem like they care about it at all. Not to mention that the main game (not World Tour) has barely recieved any costume options in the span of THREE YEARS, really shows that the company is more than happy with doing the absolute bare minimum and it is incredibly baffling. Hope things change for the better but as it stands right now, it's just not really worth playing it.

I love playing fighting games, BUT:
It's twenty twenty fking six big boss.
You DON'T just keep selling OP DLC characters for money, in an active PVP COMPETITIVE GAME.
Nerfing essentially EVERY character on features your new money crop is OP on before launching?
Are you fking serious? Game is unplayable after Alex and Ingrid.

OMFG FU Capcom.
Yall are a disgusting company that reeks of arrogance, greed and trash marketing.
Never listens or even cares to listen what players have to say about your abuse of the industry.
Charge hundreds of bucks for either some half done piece of shi or some pay to win hot trash. 
Didn't even have a chance to mention only prioritizing Japanese players and shutting down servers for other regions.
From this to DD2 and then Wilds, it only gets fking worse.

I fking bet avg age of your employees is like 58.
I hope you all go bankrupt within 5 years and sell these franchises you use to rob players for the past decade to someone who actually cares, or just shut them down idegaf anymore.
Just be gone.
Die clean, die hard and die for good with the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ yall forced players to swallow.

No longer full price for the base game which is good because its not worth it. I paid full price for a fighting game with only 12 characters. Want all of the characters thats an extra 90 bucks or more and you still have less than tekken 8s base game lineup. Street fighter has become a garbage cash grab, and for all those crybaby fanboys who eat the slop, ive been playing street fighter probably before most of you were born. This use to be my all time favorite fighting game but its no longer worth it. A 12 character base game lineup is pathetic, and im sure the roster was cut just to sell you the rest later. As a long time fan i highly recommend you pass on this until, and only if they make a complete version that includes all dlc and get it on sale. Do not support this cash grab or theyll keep doing this and it will only get worse. Do all gamers a favor and stay away from garbage like this.

Almost half the roster, you have to pay for separately from the game. I'm not a fan of gatekeeping characters or mechanics behind a paywall, so I don't recommend games that do that. If you don't care about paying extra to play the characters in game, then it's pretty fun. But if you saw they added someone you like and decided to pick it up, know you're going to be paying real money for them.

While the campaign gradually introduces you to the movesets of characters, it introduces mechanics veeeeeeeery slowly, so it's very beginner friendly, although there's still highly advanced stuff that the game doesn't really teach you. There's no avoiding the lab/youtube guides. The game definitely doesn't lack content though, World Tour is plenty long.

Year 1-2 edition is finally discounted, for me that's a good enough deal to make it my entrypoint, since I don't really care about the year 3 characters. The game is REALLY expensive otherwise if you want all the content. At the end of the day, it's still just a fighting game, I'd never pay more than 40 bucks for one.

Another multiplayer game, that 3 years into its lifespan has been turned into a monetization scam, and at the same time left at its fate against the plague of cheating that has been ruining online games since the pandemic years.


The monetization practices have been talked about for several years:
A "Create-your-character" (Avatar) system and Story Mode that look like as if they were made in the 2000s, being milked with overpriced cosmetics that are usable in modes where not even 1/10th of the active playerbase participate. Plus an in-game currency bundle scam that prevents buying even near exact amounts of the currency prices for characters, stages, cosmetics and customization options, resulting in over-spending up to twice the price of a character if bought individually and not in a character Pass.


But the worse comes for those that want to participate and improve in online modes.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Since the very release of the game, cheat software that allows for automated reactions to the opponent's inputs (by reading the game state and even having pre-programmed responses and entire bots for each character) has been created and passed around in several forums and Discords. Even consoles have been jailbroken, and devices that can be installed in them and use the same scripts as on PC are now being openly advertised even on YT.


The stance of the company: head in the sand and completely ignoring the issues for 3 years, in fact lying with empty PR statements that they are acting against cheating. In reality the game has absolutely no anticheat, and is P2P so no server-side checks or authority. 
The result is that entire regions like EU, LATAM, SE Asia, OCE are filled with cheaters using auto-react scripts and bots in EVERY ONLINE MODE! Even Casual Matches and Battle Hub have people blatantly toggling and lagswitching!

Capcom refuses to not only act on the issue on a technical level, but also has an empty report system and DO NOT EVEN DO ACCOUNT BANS ON CONFIRMED CHEATERS! Instead they do meaningless rank/point resets on those accounts, and let the cheaters go back to playing! 

On top of the above, they have been holding ONLINE QUALIFIERS for the Capcom Pro Tour, a million dollar prize tournament, ON A GAME WITHOUT ANTICHEAT!
They basically have given full pass to every cheater, and even "pros" and "online tournament orgs" that have been rife with cheating since even the days of SF5, to literally take "every chance they got". With nearly no penalties bar VERY BLATANT cases of constant auto-react botting (the cheat scripts can be toggled on / off, or to activate with a certain frequency and in specific situations).


For the past couple years the game has also been nearly abandoned with a skeleton crew (the words of the very producers of the game in interviews). Not just the anti-cheat, but even balance adjustments as well as main game content (NOT Avatar shovelware) are near nonexistent.


Conclusion
------------
SF6's success is unfortunately and largely a product of really good marketing, and due to a major number of its playerbase being situated in East Asia and naively tolerating practices that are exploitative and damaging its playability and longevity. 

If your interest in the game lies specifically in Single Player modes, wait for a sale and get it at a low price - the quality of World Tour and Avatars are not worth full price.

If however you are interested in online modes, learning and improving, treat the game and it's "pro / competitive" community in the exact same way as you would in games like CSGO, Dead By Daylight, Tarkov, Dark Souls/Elden Ring multiplayer, TF2 etc: rife with cheaters and toxicity, in a game whose very engine is completely compromised by hackers and has no anticheat.

And THEN decide if it is worth buying and playing.
(near Master ranks and above, as well as even in Casuals and Battle Hub, you cannot play without encountering handfuls of cheaters per session, unless living in the "cleaner" regions of Japan, West NA).

This game needs a balance patch terribly. The only reason I stopped playing it regularly. There are few characters absolutly doing stupid ♥♥♥♥ and playing single player game rather than 2 player game. The development team refusing to baalnce this game correctly is absolutley insult to the e-sports scene. Same characters all over high level game play. My favourite tournaments to watch became absolutely bore-shows.

Amazing game but the dlcs cost as much as a game but have less content. Why pay 30 for 4 characters every YEAR (because they barely put out outfits, events or any incentives) when you can buy a full game every year for the same price. I'd still recommend this game even though the DLCs are lacking.
if you like an easy and casual friendly street fighter experience then look no further because street fighter 6 is the game for you
The actual fighting is fine, it's everything else that's the problem. The size of the game is also insane for what it is.

just drop the price of the season 3 character pass, man. ♥♥♥♥. 

I'll change this to a positive review when I'm less mad because the game is good. but like, what are we doing here? seriously

I bought USFIV for like $40 new and it had every single character. I paid $70 for MH:Wilds and it's STILL broken on PC lmfao

capcom pls I want to love you

The people leaving a negative review for new characters they haven't even seen gameplay for, let alone PLAYED themselves is hilarious. A bunch of adult children in this community. Makoto and Dudley at one point were new faces too btw.

When this game initially came out, i could not recommend it enough. The game was fresh, it was fun, it looked great, and the new characters were a blast. Unfortunately, that game is dead. It's been replaced by horrid balance patches, overpowered characters, a drip feed of content just to get nothing of substance. Their main priority now isn't to make a good game its to put in as much crossover slop to turn a quick profit. Tifa being announced was the final nail in the  coffin. instead of giving us fan favorite characters from the street fighter franchise, we got a character to get some quick sales in. I am sick and tired of guests in games. they no longer have meaning and its more of a rule than exception. i wish SF6 could be good but if this is the route they're taking i can no longer support it. thanks for killing this game capcom

I'm more of a tekken guy, SF6 is decent at first glance especially since I'm more casual with it, but man I really wish this game was more consumer friendly cuz ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ what are those DLC prices especially for half the roster?
I'm more than happy to have a more beginner friendly SF, but really what even is this
Every season the characters offering has gone less and less interesting. And now with season 4 we got just a bunch of fillers. I am just done waiting.

I Really like the game But it costs too much to play including dlc and all those costumes and why would you put colors behind a paywall?? Also takes loads of disk space

Only for the upper class

The game is great, only major issue is that if you solely buy the game well thats actually half the game because the full roster costs more than the game itself and that price is only going to go up from here.
I'd play the game more but you're at a deficit if you dont play modern at lower ranks. It's almost not even fair if you're new and want to learn the game.
fun game but i'd rather sit in a room with a million angry spiders then through another A.K.I combo

When Street Fighter 6 came out I was very impressed with the game, it's visual flair, newcomers, and new additions to returning character's kits where great and though it had some pacing issues for me personally I had faith in Capcom's direction for the game. However I have honestly been let down, you begin every match with an already full mental stack and most games end after 3-4 significant interactions. For the top tier characters that have taken the game's balance and ran with it you are frequently guessing for game after one optimal combo. I think the power of throw loops, a lack of real significant defensive options for many characters, and a handful of characters with multiple specific monstrously over-tuned tools and buttons have made combating offensive interaction in the game a complete chore and have completely overrun the things about Street Fighter that made me fall in love with the series. The game isn't poorly put together or without love or anything, it's just not fun.
it rocks, destroys my mental in ranked but i live for the challenge and desire to improve

New characters are expensive. New characters are needed to maintain any sort of competency in multiplayer (serious long-term practicing requires ownership of said character) I have to either buy them in packs (which contain characters I do not want) or through an ingame currency (where I end up paying more than I have to, since they don't let me buy the exact amount of needed currency).

The upgrade (SF6 + Years1-2) is a cheap way to get into the game *for someone who does not own the game*. There is no further discount if you already own the game. Since Capcom cares about money more than user reviews, it shouldn't matter if a leave a bad review.
Its fun, until u get ranked with a guy from the other side of the world and you get 9999 ping and get rekt XD

Great game, pretty good for starting in. They have modern controls that make it simple to play for people with disabilities and learning issues while still having the classic controls for people that like to learn how to play the game correctly and get full enjoyment out of it.

I have already have game and 1 year character dlc but sf6 Years 1-2 Fighters Edition they are not offering a discount on the bundle and are asking $30(game + 1 year dlc + 2 year dlc); this game is very greedy for money this is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥
Just put that lazy M user with another M user, don't ruin the C player game experience

VERY fun!

Glad I gave it a chance. I think what I loved the most was the addition of the Drive Rush mechanic. Really opens up a lot of offensive options.

Over 2.5K hours on Console, switched to PC for unrelated reasons. Should you buy this game? Well let me ask you a few questions. 

Do any of the characters look cool to you? 
What about the art style? 
Do you like the graffiti aesthetic?
Do you like hip hop music?

Notice how I don’t mention anything about gameplay. Because at the end of the day, it’s a fighting game. Pro or noob, if you don’t really like any of the things I mentioned above, the game isn’t for you. Fighting games are hard, so you have to be honest with yourself about whether the things I mentioned above are worth the trials and tribulations of learning a competitive fighting game. For me, they absolutely are. 

If you do decide to pick it up, add me. “DaChocolateGuru”. I’d love to play, teach, or simply chat about the game!
Punch, lose, repeat, get gud. love it.
probably my favorite fighting game of all time, definitely in the running for being the best one Ever

best fighting game I have ever player

graphics-stuning
combat-creative and fun
figures-love them

the game is very polished


yes the dlc is expensive with the game but I rather buy a good game that worth it and pay for it instead of companies that gives you buggy and medium games.
10/10

Most fun I've had on a fighting game ever, I'd definitely recommend this if you want to get into fighting games as this one is specially easy to pick up (cough modern cough).

Also nerf ingrid please?

-out of ten game :D
This is the first fighting game I've ever owned (unless For Honor counts) and I am enjoying is tremendously.

This game is awesome, former Mortal Kombat fan. After playing this game and getting 5 characters to masters I realize just how much more fighting games really have to offer. Every mode is sick, Battlehub is cool, world tour was a good bit of fun (no more future content though so rip) and of course the main competitive mode, fighting ground is just great.
While i understand people frustrations with the DLC pricing, as a new player, being able to buy the base game at half the price feels very worth it

You know since my StarCraft II days I've been looking for something to scratch that same competitive itch but never have found something with enough mechanical precision and community swag to devote that level of time into. Maybe its the player base or the systems or even the fact that its primarily single player but I ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ love this game to a point that I worry my review is damn near useless because, realistically speaking, World Tour is fine but we're not doing MK level of cutscenes or story telling, I even think the idea of RPG fighter brawler hybrid could be done better than what they manage to get off here, there's also not a ton of self expression in a GGS way with color variations and themes, at least not without investing some kind of currency into, and as my primary fighter can I even critique it on that merit when I don't have a solid base of comparison?

lol nah fuggin 5/5 BANGER.
easy handling utk game bapak2 macam saya, dengan modern style udah cukup bwt santai2

Pros:
* Great fighting game

Cons
* $40 for the base game and then +$90 to get all of the characters. I hate how everything is being turned into a subscription model so companies can collect reoccuring revenue.
Best mordern fighting games. Period. Generally balanceed characters (various tourement winning characters shown).

One of the best fighting games of all time IMO.  Gameplay and art are excellent.  The online community is great and welcoming to beginners..  It's incredible what can happen when a developer cares about their product.

Good system based fighting game with fantastic character designs, just wish Capcom would release more skins. It's just about the only company i WANT to spend more money on. Luckily there's also no predatory practices other than prices of dlc characters.
One of the better entries in the series, some really cool new mechanics and a fun cast. Wish it wasn't so ugly but that's negligible

i love this game a ton but ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ i swear to GOD mai is possibly the worst and most annoying piece of ♥♥♥♥ added to this game. you are telling me this heathen gets a corner carry of a basic ass bread an butter along with that ♥♥♥♥♥ ass fan of hers? i have had enough. buff ryu

Too beautiful and amazing to not have ultrawide.
And one of the things I like about nearly all Japanese game menus is that they pacman tunnel on both axis.

I want to preface this review by stating I prefer arena fighters particularly ones catered to Anime brands like Dragon Ball Sparking Zero, Naruto Ninja Storm 4, Jump Force but I have put the work in through Tekken originally for games that require mechanics like these. My biggest pet peeve in SF or any fighter like these is that my hits never truly connect to one another everything is a love tab and you constantly feel your opponent out, it's just annoying. I will always prefer anything else but I do love the character designs, the animations for the super arts, and the modern schematics make it really easy to absorb game knowledge until feeling comfortable with classic. I really think the way we are forced to buy DLC is rather predatory and Tekken also knocks these guys out of the park in respect to their add-ons being directly purchasable without the in-game premium currency. Well, I can't refund this so somebody should totally teach me the way. Shoryuken time

Good netcode, fun gameplay. The modern peak of fighting games.

World Tour is a fun adventure where you go around beating up whoever dares to exist on the streets of Metro City and beyond, teaching you to do special moves disguised as minigames along the way. Your custom avatar (or somebody else's if they share their code) will level up, equip new gear and customize their moveset to some kooky mixes of fireballs, uppercuts and chunky damage grabs as you learn them from Street Fighter's cast found throughout the world. As of May 28, 2026 you can even take your custom avatars online to fight other players' crazy creations and even skip World Tour entirely if you decide to play the Avatar Arcade mode to unlock special moves and costumes.

Now that that's out of the way, the core online experience of Street Fighter 6 is fighting other players and trying to beat each other up better than the other player does. Characters range from the ever-popular all-rounder "shoto" archetype like Ryu and Ken to the giant wrestlers that will slowly approach and get one big hit for 1/4 of your life bar. Character variety improves each year but there's plenty of interesting characters to learn with iconic fighting game archetypes.

The Drive system is incredibly important: it allows you to perfectly block high and low attacks with Drive Parry but when using that parry you can still be grabbed for increased damage taken. Drive Rush will let you zoom towards your opponent and force them to let you take your turn if they don't immediately stop it (usually with a far-reaching low attack) but makes their approach predictable. Drive Impact will absorb two(!) hits of any non-Super Art attack/grab but can be countered with your opponent's own Drive Impact and cost you a lot of your precious Drive Gauge. Drive Reversal will attempt to counter with an invincible attack upon your character's recovery from being knocked down but if it's blocked or avoided you're exposed to take a punishing combo.

All in all, Street Fighter 6's Drive system is its best and worst feature, depending on who is piloting the character that expended its Drive Gauge too much to result in entering "Burnout" mode that makes you feel like you're being forced to flee or fight with one arm behind your back. Managing your Drive Gauge is key to success in this game and in my opinion it adds a layer of mastery to it that is unique to the game. Figuring out how to do a combo more efficiently in Training Room or how to patiently chip away at a blocking player's Drive Gauge has been good fun.

I really like SF6 overall. I like it's art design, I like the gameplay.
Having said that, I have some issues with the game, but I would technically recommend it to a newcomer or friend.

Personal gripes with the game:
-Flat balancing:
all the characters kinda follow the "Ken" formula on how they approach the game mechanics, it's more about which character can play SF6 better than being a different character, like I can't describe it better. Due to this flat frame data balancing the game can become very, very stale very fast since everyone kinda does the same gameplan tries to accomplish it in similar ways thanks to frame data logic being unified

-DLC Character release cadence & season pass format packaging:
Can't believe that you managed to kill my entire hype around SF6 with just some of the worst DLC character packages, I've ever seen!
Some random REDDITORS can make DLC Bundles which are more attractive to me than all the DLC packages Capcom has made for SF6, like I can't believe how fcking bad these bundles are! It's outrageously bad! Infuriatingly bad!!
The fact that Mannon, Marissa, Kimberley, Lily, Jamie all took spots from main SF characters is still pissing me off up to today!
Can't believe that some of the classic SF characters didn't make the base game!!! Then it didn't stop there, with the weird base roster no, all the DLC season passes have the weird character formated in them too! Instead of making the season passes themed or with any logic behind them, they are fckn random characters!
Now I will never get Akuma because he is in this god awful character pass bundle that I don't care about LOL
If Akuma was with Sagat, Vega & Bison (THEY SHOULD HAVE BEEN IN THE BASE GAME ROSTER ANYWAY) I'd have bought the DLC while still being pissed off about them not being in the fckn base game, at least it would be DLC pass I'd buy.
Some guy on Reddit made a post how he imagines the DLC character formats & while I don't fully agree with his choices I do think he has a point
He bundles them as following
Bundle 1: Sagat, Sakura, R.Mika + New Character
Bundle 2: Vega, Makoto, Dudley + New Character
Bundle 3: Balrog, C.Viper, Ibuki + New Character
Bundle4: Bison, Poison, Cody + New Character

WHILE I DO NOT AGREE WITH WORLD WARRIOR CHARACTERS AS DLC (Sagat, Akuma, Vega, Bison, Balrog etc)
I'd have bought each of these bundles because they all include multiple characters I'm interested in
Meanwhile Capcom can't fckng assemble a single season pass I'm interested in, it's baffling! & Then I look at the pricing and get more angry & never come back to playing SF6.

That's it, that's what I had to say about my personal gripes.

EVERYTHING ELSE IS GOOD about the game. Online functionality, single player content, lobby system, clan system etc etc. I like the game!

Love that they added old & new designs of the same characters! I just hope that they will never completely redesign the old characters! If you want to make new character designs make new characters & maybe people like them or they don't, but don't alter the existing SF cast.

STOPPED PLAYING CAMMY/CAMMY FIGHTING GAME CUS DLC FORMAT MADE ME WANT TO CHOKE PEOPLE DEAD (and it's a bit stale game, very rigid)

Final Verdict: 8/10

Best fighting game ive ever played. The character balance feels really good, and mechanics like footsies, cross-ups, and anti-airs all feel rewarding and fair. The Drive system is such a fresh addition and makes every match exciting. Performance is super smooth too, even online matches against players from the other side of the world feel great to play.

Ridiculously high skill ceiling, but in ranked I'm slumming it down in rookie after 30 hours, and I'm enjoying myself. The story mode is pretty fun too, and I think it's a decent way to get into fighting games. 
  
Overall, as a complete noob, I'm enjoying it a lot more than I thought I would.

Great fighting game for everyone to enjoy!

Okay, I haven't really been into Street Fighter my whole life, as I was a huge Tekken and Mortal Kombat fan. I've played Street Fighter here and there with my dad, uncles, and cousins, so I have an average grasp of how the games in the series play and work (I am not a good SF player by any means, but I'm learning).

But holy ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ♥♥♥♥, SF6 opened my eyes to how good the series actually is! I was able to apply my knowledge from older SF games to this one, so it was kind of easy for me to pick up and learn the game.

Online play also seems to be way better than in previous SF games like SFV and USFIV, so kudos to Capcom for giving this game decent netcode.

However, my issue is that there is a lack of actual costumes in-game, as you have to buy them with real money. But oh well—the skins are fire, so why not lol.

Anyways, great overall experience. 10/10 :))

While i may not be the biggest fan of the drive system, i have to admit that the game is polished and (mostly) well thought out. Afaik, this is the SF game with the most single player content and most well-paced introduction to its mechanics to date. This is huge when you compare it to its predecesor SFV, so, good on you capcom.
That being said, the DLC business model its flat out ridiculous, its way too expensive and it basically punishes anyone who bought the game at launch and not the fighters edition.
In classic Capcom fashion, youre not allowed to lab game characters on practice mode like in other games (2xko) in order to make you pay a hefty ammount for characters that you dont really wanna play.
All in all, i wish there was a "game good, company bad" button on steam.
I've never been very good at fighting games, but I played a lot of street fighter growing up despite that. This is the first SF since SFIII:3rd strike that I've genuinely enjoyed playing.

I bought SF6 for the World Tour mode, where you build a character, travel around the world looking for an answer to "what is strength", learn moves from SF characters, and get involved in various conflicts.

It's a bold concept for a fighting game, but the execution is... meh.

The pros: there's a whole RPG in there. You have levels, gear, skill trees, quests and collectibles. You can challenge most NPCs to fights, while some NPCs will attack you on sight. You can use some moves in the open world to break objects, move across chasms, or gain height. All of these somehow work on top of a fighting game, and it's novel.

The cons: the story is very meh. You have a training buddy Bosch, who raised like a million death flags in the first hour. You part ways very early, then you go around the world and do stuff. I don't remember any of that stuff, especially the first 2/3. The story doesn't really put weight into anything, you do things because a quest giver / message asks you to, with rarely enough context on why, or what happens afterwards, you just move on to the next thing. The ending is extremely anticlimactic as well.

You can learn like a hundred moves from various masters, but when equipping them, you obviously can't equip any two with the same command. Then you realize the game doesn't provide a way to sort moves by input commands.

Also it took me 20 hours to discover I have a skill to recover health outside of fights; before that I always assumed I had to either eat or level up.

Based on the World Tour mode alone, I think it's worth buying when on sale and play this RPG for 30 hours, but after clearing the story I have lost all interest in doing the post game stuff.
Not a perfect game by any means but it is very fun, now all they need to do is nerf JP and we'll be golden.

Great. For a first fighting game other than brawlhalla, not too terribleto get into, although classic is still kinda difficult.

While I did and still do like the game it was so frustrating and disheartening losing constantly to "Modern" control players who could flawlessly pull off insane combos I could only dream of pulling off after hours of practice using "Classic" controls. 

Some might say I should switch to modern controls if I'm finding it so difficult to face modern control players but it wouldnt be a problem if I could just face classic control players, but unfortunately there is no way to filter out these troglodite modern control players, so I can't really recommend it if you're someone looking for a more authentic fighting game experience.

Despite the hate you'd find surrounding the game, it's really well done. It's like the first fighting game I've ever played outside of the normal Mortal Kombat/Tekken mash with cousins as a kid. I bought the game on December 2025 and have been hooked ever since.

I forced myself onto Classic and now I don't really regret it, but it was a very rough start. I spent a few weeks in rookie (the lowest rank in the game), eating ♥♥♥♥ every single game, losing to literally the worst players in the game. The moment the inputs clicked, the game made sense, and the few guide videos I watched made sense I started climbing, and I'm now grateful I didn't start with Modern, but that's just how I played the game. 

If you're scared from this beginning period of eating ♥♥♥♥ over and over and having your ego dismantled then Modern is YOUR path. The difference between the two isn't really present unless in really high levels and even there you can find Modern players competing and reaching high levels. So don't overthink it really.

SF6 is a treasure, and I hope Capcom doesn't do any rash changes when the game's really polished and fun. If you're on the fence about buying the game then do yourself a favor and snag it. If you're still hesitant and unsure then do yourself even more of a favor and play Modern. Trust me Classic is only for the people who are ready to have their ego dismantled and won't get discouraged by a few weeks of getting rekt left, right, and center.

Please, do us a favor, and make the FGC bigger. Buy this game, and talk to your friend group and rope them into this too #notsponsored. The only bad thing about SF6 and the FGC in general is the obvious lower numbers of players, don't get me wrong, SF6 is thriving at the timing of this review's release, but it is nowhere near the level the game deserves. Drop whatever MOBA, FPS, Gacha, or Battle Royale game you've been grinding and hop onto Fighting Games, where the only person to blame for your loss is you and the balance team, say goodbye to those godawful teammates, trolls, and toxic players. It's you and your teabagger opponent, nothing more, nothing less.

So I had a good time playing this when it first came out, it's been a few years and I've been hearing good things, trying to get into fighting games again. Anyway, what the ♥♥♥♥ is this dlc system? Why do I need to spend 12$ to get a character I want, and be left with 260FC, not even enough for a costume? 

I'm interested in A.K.I and no other characters from pack 1, Mai and Terry from Pack 2, and Viper and Ingrid from 3, but instead of being able to just spend $7 and get the character like most fighting games now I need to either buy fake ass currency that screws you out of even amounts, or buy a bunch of characters I have no interest in. I guess I'll wait for a possible Super street fighter 6 before touching this series. ♥♥♥♥ off with the 250FC, 610FC, 1250FC, and 2750FC ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ that leaves you with worthless leftovers no matter what. The ♥♥♥♥♥♥ PPV tournament is another way of Capcom ripping off it's customers. Glad to see that now you aren't making ♥♥♥♥♥♥ games anymore you decide to rip everyone off cause people like you again.

They took the soul of Street Fighter and turned it into a button masher.  Modern layout controls remove the skill and make the game repetitive.  
Arcade mode is soulless and feels like the computer is cheating.  The computer countering six throws in a row just feels bad.  Simply holding down the assist button and mashing your way to victory is boring and unfulfilling.      

World tour mode is even worse.  Your skill doesn't matter when the computer has no reaction time lag is able to perfectly counter and hits like a truck.  Fighting without using Skyrim tactics (pausing and eating) is a quick way to get frustrated.  Your character feels weak and underpowered even compared to same level mooks.  They also use the tired trope of invisible walls and put quests just on the other side.  You can see them, you just can't do them because reasons.  

If you're looking for a hit of nostalgia, this isn't it.  Pass on this game and go for something else with some skill.

Pros:
-Good graphics
-You always feel like a genius when you win footsies off of a good read
-Frame data is built such that you don't end up stuck in blockstrings that make you feel like you're watching a MHW cutscene
-It's street fighter, what do you expect? It's got good neutral. If you want hyperoffense and crazy characters go play SkullGirls or Guilty Gear or something
Cons:
-I hope whoever thought of Drive Impact feels pain in hell equal to what that ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ mechanic caused every player who just wanted to play neutral
-New Fighting Game players can very easily learn bad habits because blocking is actively penalized
-Those two things alone make me hesitant to say "I recommend this" but since there's no neutral option I'm leaving the thumbs up for now
This game is absolutely awesome! If you're thinking about getting into fighting games definitely pick this one up!
